notes: Codex /clear adaptation. Codex's Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.codex/sessions): a proc-<PID> pre-session appears at launch, hands off to a rollout-keyed UUID-1 session on the first prompt, runs one turn, then /clear + a second prompt spawns a SECOND rollout (UUID-2) with a fresh session_id and no UUID-1 history. The strongest observable proxy for the reset is v2_session_birth (new_session:true) — a rollout-keyed session_id never seen before. UNLIKE claudecode, codex does NOT emit transcript_removed for the old session at /clear time (the old rollout lingers, frozen, until process_exited / readyTTL sweep), so there is deliberately NO old_session_ended phase here — asserting one would demand an event codex never produces. KNOWN CAPTURE GAP: the onboarding codex driver caches the first rollout and writes a single session.uuid, so curate-lifecycle-fixture.sh filters the fixture to UUID-1 only; the v2_* phases assert what the daemon observes live but may not be present in the single-session curated fixture, surfacing as validation drift (commit-and-flag) until the codex driver tracks /clear-spawned sessions.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of codex launch
After the first prompt lands, the rollout-keyed session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh UUID
First user prompt drives ready → working on the rollout session
First turn completes back to ready (same rollout UUID)

Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — after /clear, the first post-clear prompt spawns a fresh rollout UUID never seen before; this is the strongest observable proxy for 'the conversation was reset'
Second user prompt drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1)
Second turn completes back to ready on the new session
